title: Elderly medical deduction likely under-applied
explanation: Bà Nguyễn is 72 years old with $250/month in documented medical expenses for hypertension and arthritis. Only the $35 threshold is excluded — the remaining $215/month is a full dollar-for-dollar deduction from net income, increasing monthly SNAP by approximately $64. This deduction is frequently missed for elderly single-person households.
suggestedAction: Verify all out-of-pocket medical costs: prescriptions, copays, medical transport, over-the-counter items prescribed by a doctor. Apply the full excess amount ($215) as a deduction.

title: Senior Farmers Market Nutrition Program (SFMNP) not flagged
explanation: Bà Nguyễn qualifies for DC's Senior Farmers Market Nutrition Program — a $50 seasonal voucher for fresh produce at farmers markets including Eastern Market. This program is separate from SNAP and WIC and requires a separate enrollment that caseworkers often forget to initiate.
suggestedAction: Enroll in DC SFMNP through the Office on Aging. Eastern Market and several H St NE vendors accept SFMNP vouchers — close to her address at 619 H St NE.
